Center: Center of Nuclear Physics Data, Russian Federal Nuclear Center (VNIIEF), Sarov, Russia
title: Investigation of the Mg-24(alpha,gamma)Si-28 reaction and of some Al-27(p,gamma)Si-28 resonances

x4freetext: Investigation of the Mg-24(alpha,gamma)Si-28 reaction
and of some Al-27(p,gamma)Si-28 resonances
The current, varying between 3 and
8 microA, was largest at the higher energies.
NaI scintillation crystals were 10 cm long and
10 cm in diameter.
REACTION code in Sub 002,007 was changed,
COMMON in Sub 002,007 was added. Sample description in
Sub 002-004 was corrected.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. Subent 005
deleted (out of EXFOR scope).
Number of counts
Singly charged helium ions.
Targets have been used of natural magnesium, and of
enriched Mg-24, both evaporated onto copper backings.
Three different targets were used for different alpha
energy ranges. For E < 3.1 MeV an enriched Mg-24
target of 15 ug/cm2 was used; for E > 3.1 MeV the
target was natural Mg of 5 ug/cm2 thickness; in the
energy range of approximately from 2.7 MeV to 2.93 MeV
a natural Mg target of about 3 ug/cm2 thickness was
used.
NaI scintillation crystal, 10 cm long and
10 cm in iameter. It was placed as close to the target
as possible.
Alpha particle energy was determined with 90 degrees
deflecting magnet, in which the orbit radius amounted
to 31 cm. The magnetic field was measured with a Li-7
magnetic resonance fluxmeter.
400-channel pulse analyser was used.
Fig.2
SD: BIB updated.

x4freetext: Singly charged helium ions.
Targets have been used of natural magnesium, and of
enriched Mg-24, both evaporated onto copper backings.
Three different targets were used for different alpha
energy ranges. For E < 3.1 MeV an enriched Mg-24
target of 15 mu-g/cm2 was used; for E > 3.1 Mev the
target was natural Mg of 5 mu-g/cm2 thickness; in the
energy range of approximately from 2.7 Mev to 2.93 MeV
a natural Mg target of about 3 mu-g/cm2 thickness was
used.
NaI scintillation crystal, 10 cm long and
10 cm in iameter. It was placed as close to the target
as possible.
Alpha particle energy was determined with 90 degrees
deflecting magnet, in which the orbit radius amounted
to 31 cm. The magnetic field was measured with a Li7
magnetic resonance fluxmeter.
400-channel pulse analyser
The data obtained from the 24Mg(alpha,gamma)28Si
reaction were compared with the information from the
27Al(p,gamma)28Si and 27Al(p,alpha)24Mg reactions.
From the measured yields of these three reactions,
the partial widths for alpha, p and gamma are computed
for the most resonance levels.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The errors assigned to the (alpha,gamma)
resonance energies are compounded from:
a) the contribution, proportional to Ealpha, resulting
from the error in the calibration energy;
b) the error, also taken proportional to Ealpha, in the
determination of the low-energy edge of a resonance
peak;
c) the estimated error due to magnet saturation.
The errors in resonance strength might amount to 30%
at the strong resonances, and up to a factor of two at
the weakest resonances.
Table II

Singly charged helium ions.
Targets have been used of natural magnesium, and of
enriched Mg-24, both evaporated onto copper backings.
Three different targets were used for different alpha
energy ranges. For E < 3.1 MeV an enriched Mg-24
target of 15 ug/cm2 was used; for E > 3.1 MeV the
target was natural Mg of 5 ug/cm2 thickness; in the
energy range of approximately from 2.7 MeV to 2.93 MeV
a natural Mg target of about 3 ug/cm2 thickness was
used.
Alpha particle energy was determined with 90 degrees
deflecting magnet, in which the orbit radius amounted
to 31 cm. The magnetic field was measured with a Li-7
magnetic resonance fluxmeter.
Two NaI crystals were used to detect gamma
radiation.
Normalization from least-squares analysis. Background
has been subtracted.
Any excentricity of the
target spot with respect to the center of the counter
turntable was corrected by calibrating on isotropic
622 keV resonance.
Distance between target and front of
crystal D=10cm
Distance between target and front of
crystal D=5 cm
Initial abscissa data are given in COS**2.
